basics.name: Remzi Baris Tercioglu
basics.label: Economist
basics.summary: Economist at the World Bank focused on macroeconomic modeling, climate macroeconomics, fiscal policy, and forecasting.

work.company: World Bank, Macro and Debt Unit, Economic Policy Global Practice
work.summary: Develop and apply semi-structural macroeconomic models for stabilization policy, global imbalances, and international spillovers.
highlights: Design stochastic simulation frameworks to assess climate, fiscal, and external risks under alternative policy and global shock scenarios.
Contribute to the architecture of medium-term macro-fiscal projection systems used by World Bank country teams and Ministries of Finance.
Provide technical training to Ministries of Finance in Argentina, Chile, and Mexico on macroeconomic modeling and forecasting.

work.company: World Bank, Jobs and Economic Growth Unit, Economic Policy Global Practice
work.summary: Developed multi-sector semi-structural macroeconomic models to quantify the macroeconomic and labor market impacts of climate transition policies.
highlights: Implemented counterfactual policy simulations for Country Climate and Development Reports covering Sri Lanka, Belize, the Kyrgyz Republic, and Maldives.

work.company: World Bank, Fiscal Policy and Sustainable Growth Unit, Macroeconomics, Trade and Investment Global Practice
work.summary: Designed and estimated open-economy DSGE models using Bayesian methods to assess climate-fiscal reforms and net-zero transition strategies in emerging markets.
highlights: Performed quantitative policy analysis and counterfactual simulations under alternative fiscal consolidation and carbon pricing scenarios.

work.company: World Bank, Chief Economist Office, Equitable Growth, Finance and Institutions Vice Presidency
work.summary: Estimated fiscal multipliers using time-series and panel econometric methods.
highlights: Contributed to the methodological development of the Climate Policy Assessment Tool (CPAT) for evaluating the macroeconomic impacts of climate policies.

education.institution: New School for Social Research, New York
courses: Dissertation: A Sectoral Approach to Measuring U.S. Growth, Inequality, and the Output Gap
